content.claim: The fastest-growing SEN subcategory is 'severe learning, attention, or behaviour-regulation disorder' (BTMN-adjacent), which comprised 45,300 students in 2021/22 (12% year-on-year increase) and approximately 69% of integrated SEN students, representing roughly 50% growth over ten years—compared to slower growth in sensory/motor/intellectual disabilities.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: 'Soft' diagnostic categories are more sensitive to changes in assessment practice and referral behavior, making attribution to true prevalence vs. practice change difficult
no formal decomposition of diagnostic-modernization vs. legal-change vs. real-prevalence drivers is available in cited sources
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
